catalog_content:
Item Name: Sri Thai Jasmine Rice 20-Pound
Bullet Point 1: White rice is milled rice that has had its husk, bran, and germ removed. This alters the flavor, texture and appearance of the rice and helps prevent spoilage and extend its storage life.
Bullet Point 2: After milling, the rice is polished, resulting in a seed with a bright, white, shiny appearance.
Bullet Point 3: The milling and polishing processes both remove nutrients. A diet based on unenriched white rice leaves many people vulnerable to the neurological disease beriberi, due to a deficiency of thiamine (vitamin B1)
Bullet Point 4: White rice is often enriched with some of the nutrients stripped from it during its processing. Enrichment of white rice with B1, B3, and iron is required by law in the United States.
Bullet Point 5: As with all natural foods, the precise nutritional composition of rice varies slightly depending on the variety, soil conditions, environmental conditions and types of fertilizers
Product Description: Adopted over brown rice in the second half of the 19th century because it was favored by traders, white rice has led to a beriberi epidemic in Asia. At various times, starting in the 19th century, brown rice and wild rice have been advocated as more healthful alternatives. The bran in brown rice contains significant dietary fiber and the germ contains many vitamins and minerals. Typically, 100 grams of uncooked rice produces around 240 to 260 grams of cooked grains, the difference in weight being due to absorbed cooking water. Brown Jasmine Rice
Value: 320.0
Unit: Ounce
overall_summary: Mid-tier imported rice with a premium scent-driven appeal but no explicit organic or specialty certifications. The 20-lb bulk packaging suggests affordability per unit weight, targeting budget-conscious consumers while leveraging Thailand’s reputation for high-quality jasmine rice. Standard plastic packaging and lack of premium branding cues position it as a value-driven commodity rather than a luxury product.
